title: useNullishCoalescing

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="JavaScript (and super languages)" icon="seti:javascript">
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
:::note
This rule belongs to the types domain. This means that its activation will activate the Biome Scanner to scan the files of your project, and enable the type inference engine. Read more about it in the [documentation page](/linter/domains#types)
:::
## Summary
- Rule available since: `v2.4.5`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/useNullishCoalescing`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule has a [**safe**](/linter/#safe-fixes) fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
- This rule belongs to the following domains:
  - [`types`](/linter/domains#types)
- Sources: 
  - Inspired from [`@typescript-eslint/prefer-nullish-coalescing`](https://typescript-eslint.io/rules/prefer-nullish-coalescing)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useNullishCoalescing": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Enforce using the nullish coalescing operator (`??`) instead of logical or (`||`).

`??` only checks for `null` and `undefined`, while `||` checks for any falsy value
including `0`, `''`, and `false`. The rule reports `||`, `||=`, and ternary patterns
(`x !== null ? x : y`) when type analysis shows the left operand is possibly nullish.

## Examples

### Invalid

```ts title='invalid-or.ts'
declare const maybeString: string | null;
const value = maybeString || 'default';
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///invalid-or.ts">/invalid-or.ts</a>:2:27 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-nullish-coalescing">lint/nursery/useNullishCoalescing</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Use </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> instead of </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">.</span><br />  <br />    <strong>1 │ </strong>declare const maybeString: string | null;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>const value = maybeString || 'default';<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>                          <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>3 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> operator checks for all falsy values (including 0, '', and false), while </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> only checks for null and undefined.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule is still being actively worked on, so it may be missing features or have rough edges. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043">https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information or to report possible bugs.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

```ts title='invalid-or-undefined.ts'
declare const maybeNumber: number | undefined;
const value = maybeNumber || 0;
```

```ts title='invalid-or-assign.ts'
declare let x: string | null;
x ||= 'default';
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///invalid-or-assign.ts">/invalid-or-assign.ts</a>:2:3 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-nullish-coalescing">lint/nursery/useNullishCoalescing</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Use </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??=</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> instead of </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||=</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">.</span><br />  <br />    <strong>1 │ </strong>declare let x: string | null;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>x ||= 'default';<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>3 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||=</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> operator assigns when the left side is falsy, while </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??=</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> only assigns when it is null or undefined.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule is still being actively worked on, so it may be missing features or have rough edges. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043">https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information or to report possible bugs.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

```ts
declare const x: string | null;
const value = x !== null ? x : 'default';
```

```ts
declare const x: string | null;
const value = x == null ? 'default' : x;
```

/></code></pre>

### Valid

```ts
declare const maybeString: string | null;
const value = maybeString ?? 'default';
```

```ts
declare const definiteString: string;
const value = definiteString || 'fallback';
```

```ts
declare const cond: string | null;
if (cond || 'fallback') {
  console.log('in if');
}
```

```ts
declare let y: string | null;
y ??= 'default';
```

## Options

### ignoreConditionalTests

Ignore `||` expressions inside conditional test positions (if/while/for/do-while/ternary).
Default: `true`.

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useNullishCoalescing": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"ignoreConditionalTests": false
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```ts
declare const cond: string | null;
if (cond || 'fallback') {}
```

### ignoreTernaryTests

Ignore ternary expressions that check for `null` or `undefined`. Default: `false`.

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useNullishCoalescing": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"ignoreTernaryTests": true
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```ts
declare const x: string | null;
const value = x !== null ? x : 'default';
```

### ignoreMixedLogicalExpressions

Ignore `||` and `||=` whose connected logical tree also contains a `&&`. Default: `false`.

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useNullishCoalescing": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"ignoreMixedLogicalExpressions": true
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

#### Invalid

`||` and `||=` are still reported when the surrounding logical tree does not contain `&&`.

```ts title='invalid-mixed-or.ts'
declare const maybeString: string | null;
const value = maybeString || 'default';
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///invalid-mixed-or.ts">/invalid-mixed-or.ts</a>:2:27 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-nullish-coalescing">lint/nursery/useNullishCoalescing</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Use </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> instead of </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">.</span><br />  <br />    <strong>1 │ </strong>declare const maybeString: string | null;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>const value = maybeString || 'default';<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>                          <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>3 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> operator checks for all falsy values (including 0, '', and false), while </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> only checks for null and undefined.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule is still being actively worked on, so it may be missing features or have rough edges. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043">https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information or to report possible bugs.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

```ts title='invalid-mixed-or-assign.ts'
declare let assigned: string | null;
assigned ||= 'default';
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///invalid-mixed-or-assign.ts">/invalid-mixed-or-assign.ts</a>:2:10 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-nullish-coalescing">lint/nursery/useNullishCoalescing</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Use </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??=</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> instead of </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||=</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">.</span><br />  <br />    <strong>1 │ </strong>declare let assigned: string | null;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>assigned ||= 'default';<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>         <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>3 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||=</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> operator assigns when the left side is falsy, while </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??=</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> only assigns when it is null or undefined.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule is still being actively worked on, so it may be missing features or have rough edges. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043">https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information or to report possible bugs.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

#### Valid

`||` and `||=` mixed with `&&` in the same logical tree are not reported.

```ts
declare const a: string | null;
declare const b: string;
const r = (a || 'default') && b;
```

```ts
declare const b: string;
declare let assigned: string | null;
assigned ||= b && 'fallback';
```

### ignoreBooleanCoercion

Ignore `||` and `||=` used inside a `Boolean()` call, where coalescing on
falsy values is intentional. Default: `false`.

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useNullishCoalescing": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"ignoreBooleanCoercion": true
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

#### Invalid

`||` and `||=` outside a `Boolean()` call are still reported.

```ts title='invalid-boolean-coercion.ts'
declare const maybeString: string | null;
const value = maybeString || 'default';
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///invalid-boolean-coercion.ts">/invalid-boolean-coercion.ts</a>:2:27 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-nullish-coalescing">lint/nursery/useNullishCoalescing</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Use </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> instead of </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">.</span><br />  <br />    <strong>1 │ </strong>declare const maybeString: string | null;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>const value = maybeString || 'default';<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>                          <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>3 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> operator checks for all falsy values (including 0, '', and false), while </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> only checks for null and undefined.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule is still being actively worked on, so it may be missing features or have rough edges. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043">https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information or to report possible bugs.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

#### Valid

`||` and `||=` inside a `Boolean()` call are not reported.

```ts title='valid-boolean-coercion.ts'
declare const a: string | null;
declare const b: string;
const r = Boolean(a || b);
```

### ignorePrimitives

Ignore `||`, `||=`, and ternary expressions when every non-nullish variant
of the operand is a primitive the option opts out of. Use `true` to ignore
all primitives, or an object selecting `string`, `number`, `boolean`, or
`bigint`. Default: none.

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useNullishCoalescing": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"ignorePrimitives": {
							"string": true
						}
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

#### Invalid

Primitive kinds that are not opted out of are still reported.

```ts title='invalid-primitives.ts'
declare const count: number | null;
const value = count || 0;
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///invalid-primitives.ts">/invalid-primitives.ts</a>:2:21 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-nullish-coalescing">lint/nursery/useNullishCoalescing</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Use </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> instead of </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">.</span><br />  <br />    <strong>1 │ </strong>declare const count: number | null;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>const value = count || 0;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>                    <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>3 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>||</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> operator checks for all falsy values (including 0, '', and false), while </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>??</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> only checks for null and undefined.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule is still being actively worked on, so it may be missing features or have rough edges. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043">https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/8043</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information or to report possible bugs.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

#### Valid

A `string` operand is not reported when `string` is ignored.

```ts title='valid-primitives.ts'
declare const name: string | null;
const value = name || 'default';
```
